definition: Core goods CPI at -1% to +0.5% represents the exhaustion of supply-normalization-driven disinflation, with asymmetric risk tilted toward re-inflation from tariffs (0.3-0.8pp direct CPI impact), deglobalization friction, and structural commodity price floors.
evidence: Core goods CPI at -1% to +0.5% reflects resolution of identifiable supply shocks
Three-phase sequencing template places economy in late Phase 2 / early Phase 3
Tariff impact estimated at 0.3-0.8pp direct CPI via Amiti-Redding-Weinstein 2019 calibration
Pattern consistent across all 6 historical episodes studied
open_questions: How large will the tariff-driven goods re-inflation be?
Are markets correctly distinguishing supply-normalization from demand-destruction disinflation?
